message:
🌱 kyverno: [Feature] Extend `foreach` to cover generate rules (#1051)

* 🌱 Add kyverno: [Feature] Extend `foreach` to cover generate rules mission

Signed-off-by: github-actions[bot] <41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: correct PR URL for kyverno#3542 foreach for generate rules

PR #4386 was closed without merge. PR #10875 (merged Aug 2024)
is the actual fix for extending foreach to cover generate rules.

Signed-off-by: Andy Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

---------

Signed-off-by: github-actions[bot] <41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>
Signed-off-by: Andy Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>
Co-authored-by: github-actions[bot] <41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

+  "mission": {
+    "title": "kyverno: [Feature] Extend `foreach` to cover generate rules",
+    "description": "[Feature] Extend `foreach` to cover generate rules. Requested by 12+ users.",
+    "type": "feature",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Check current kyverno deployment",
+        "description": "Verify your kyverno version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kyverno -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kyverno
helm list -n kyverno 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working kyverno installation."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Review kyverno configuration",
+        "description": "Inspect the relevant kyverno configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n kyverno -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kyverno
kubectl get configmap -n kyverno -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=kyverno
```
### Problem Statement

The `foreach` ability covers validate and mutate rules, but not generate."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Apply the fix for [Feature] Extend `foreach` to cover generate rules",
+        "description": "Extends foreach to cover generate rules.
Done 👍🏽 

### Proof Manifests

If the pollicy given below is applied then multiple configMap will get created on the creation of a pod:

```yaml
apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1
kind: ClusterPolicy
metadata:
  name: basic-policy2
spec:
  rules:
  - name:
```yaml
apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1
kind: ClusterPolicy
metadata:
  name: basic-policy2
spec:
  rules:
  - name: test-foreach-generate
    match:
      any:
      - resources:
          kinds:
          - Pod
    generate:
      foreach:
      - list: "request.object.spec.containers"
        kind: ConfigMap
        apiVersion: v1
        name: "custom-created-configmap"
        namespace: "{{request.object.metadata.namespace}}"
        synchronize: false
        data:
          data:
            foo: "bar is my container name"
      - list: "request.object.spec.containers"
        kind: ConfigMap
      
```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Verify the feature works",
+        "description": "Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kyverno -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kyverno
kubectl get events -n kyverno --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "[Feature] Extend `foreach` to cover generate rules" is functioning correctly."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "Extends foreach to cover generate rules.
Done 👍🏽

```yaml

kind: ClusterPolicy
metadata:
  name: basic-policy2
spec:
  rules:
  - name: test-foreach-generate
    match:
      any:
      - resources:
          kinds:
          - Pod
   ",
